coverage.name: ProShares 3x Nasdaq-100
coverage.thesis: **The book's 3x core — held behind the brake into tomorrow's CPI.** 10 sh @ $76.29, spot **~$74.34 (-2.6%, day -3.6%)** as chips lead a 2nd day of risk-off (SOXL -9%; Samsung's miss + a report DeepSeek is building its own AI chip + profit-taking after +80% H1). The tell: QQQ slipped under its 20-day (~$722.6) but is **defending its 50-day (~$716)** after bouncing off the $712.6 low — a dip-to-support test, not a decisive break — and AI-capex keeps confirming (TSMC June +68% YoY). No trail this run: still a down day, no new higher-low to ratchet to; stop stays **$68.50 GTC** (below the 7/8 swing low $69.57, ~8% under spot). Tuesday 8:30am **CPI** is the binary — headline seen cooling to ~3.9% on -10% gasoline, core sticky ~2.9%; the $68.50 stop is the defined downside on a break.
coverage.size_note: 10 sh held — the single 3x core, ~76% of sleeve, $68.50 GTC stop

coverage.name: Direxion 3x Semis
coverage.verdict_label: Rotation candidate · gate UNMET · bear hedge on standby
coverage.thesis: **Semis under trend — long gate UNMET, and I won't short into a print consensus sees cooling.** SOXL ~$168 (-12% today) — the semi basket is well under its 20-day, so no TQQQ->SOXL flip. The **both-directions** read: the clean bear expression (**SQQQ ~$39.4 / SOXS ~$4.59**, both already +4-12% today) would pay on a hot CPI, but chasing inverses that have already run, the day before a number expected to cool, is fighting the base case — a hedge armed only if CPI runs hot and price confirms a breakdown, not a position today.
coverage.size_note: 3x semis — **~−9% pre-market** on the SK Hynix (−8%) / Micron (−4%) drag. Confirms the sector's the weak spot today; no long here, and not chasing the SOXS spike into a CPI binary either.

coverage.name: GraniteShares 2x NVDA
coverage.thesis: **2x NVDA — still the book's best position on entry (+4.4%).** 1 sh @ $30.59, spot **~$31.94** (day -3.2% with the chip tape, but +4.4% on our entry). Rides NVDA (~$207, -1.8%) holding up far better than the broad semi basket (SOXL -9%) — relative strength + TSMC June +68% YoY confirming AI-capex. Lone leftover single share; **$29.50 GTC stop** (~7.6% under) locks most of the gain. Too small to add to (wash-blocked, no BP) — it just rides its stop.
coverage.size_note: 1 sh held — 2x NVDA, ~3% of sleeve, $29.50 GTC stop

coverage.name: Nvidia
coverage.verdict_label: dust flushed at open ✓ — full size in paper (130 sh)
coverage.thesis: **Leader holding ~$208 (day -1.6%, off the $211 record) while the broad chip basket craters (SOXL -9%) — real relative strength.** AI-hardware demand keeps confirming (TSMC June +68% YoY; bullish falling-wedge per 7/11). The tiny 0.05sh live dust **market-sold at the 9:30 open** per the charter's cleanup rule — confirmed done (cash $8.88->$19.59), so every remaining live position is protected. Full-size NVDA exposure lives in the paper book (130 sh @ $210.26, $186 GTC stop).
coverage.size_note: 0.05sh dust — flushed at the open ✓, cash freed

coverage.name: Constellation Energy

No fresh catalyst and it'll dip with the tape today; a clean break with the AI-power bid makes it a ~$200 vehicle. Bench watch — not competing with the deployed book.

coverage.thesis: **AI data-center infra, ~$319.** Faded from the $338 spike, digesting. Strong secular power/cooling story but no fresh trigger; watch for a reclaim of $330+ to re-engage.

coverage.name: Tesla
coverage.thesis: **On the practice book — filled at the open, basing ~$395.62 (-3.5% today, -3.5% on entry).** Reclaimed a bullish signal at $394; Q2 deliveries (480,126, a beat) already out — no imminent earnings landmine. 36 sh @ $410.10 behind the **$348 GTC stop** (-15%, below the $394 reclaim). A clean non-chip name diversifying the paper book off its NVDA/semis tilt. LIVE: no position — a whole share (~$396) needs a core sale to fund the ~$1k sleeve; re-engage on a clear >$415 push.

coverage.name: SoFi
coverage.thesis: **High-beta held — but the relative-strength tell faded today.** 10 sh @ $18.76, spot **~$18.32 (-2.3%, day -2.5%)** — SoFi opened green ($19.23 high) on its recent strength (Goldman $21 target, ARK buying, the Composer deal) but handed the pop back and is now red with the tape, so the book has no leader today. Still above its **$17.60 GTC stop** (below the 7/9 higher-low $17.71, ~4% under spot). Not cutting it into weakness the day before CPI — the stop is the discipline; if the relative strength doesn't reassert after the print, it's the first rotation candidate.
coverage.size_note: 10 sh held — high-beta, ~19% of sleeve, $17.60 GTC stop

coverage.name: Bloom Energy
coverage.thesis: **No floor — avoid.** Violent two-way ranges all last week (Fri o $251 → low $229.6 → close $244.7, −4.8%). No stable structure to buy; sidelined until it bases.

Re-ticket only on a genuine reclaim of strength (>$86) — not catching the knife into $79–82, and not into a risk-off pre-CPI tape.

10:47am — held the whole book, nothing to approve. 10:14am — an hour into the session, held the whole book. I'd only move if the core breaks its trend or a real new setup shows up.

11:40am — quiet **hold**, everything protected, nothing for you to do. The tape's narrow: **Nvidia** is basically the only thing working (up ~3% to new highs on China clearing 400k+ of its top chips), and it's carrying the whole index — under the surface the quantum, nuclear, space and Palantir names are all red, and Bloom's down 10%. That actually suits us: our little **2x-Nvidia** piece is our best position (+6%) doing the day's work, the **SoFi** buy is green on its own news (it's acquiring an AI-trading startup, Composer), and the 3x-Nasdaq core is flat, riding the trend. Book's up a couple dollars; all three safety exits re-checked at the broker (core $65, Nvidia $27, SoFi $16.80). No cash left to add and no clean new trade worth making, so I'm just letting the winners run.

Caught why the **SoFi tap didn't go through**: you approved it, but the broker rejected it for **insufficient buying power** — a marketable-limit buy reserves *shares × limit price* up front, and 10 × $19.90 = $199 just topped our $196 cash. My fault on the sizing. I **re-issued** the exact same idea sized to fit (10 sh, limit $19.05 → reserves $190), so your next tap fills cleanly. Hard exit $16.80, risk ~$19. Nothing else changed — core's still long and protected.

The Nvidia story firmed up and it's **bigger than the first headline**: China didn't just *consider* it — it **approved** ByteDance, Alibaba and Tencent to buy **400,000+** of Nvidia's top H200 chips (signed off during Jensen Huang's visit this week). That's why Nvidia's +2% and our little 2x position is our best holding. Under the surface though, today's **risk-off**: chips, quantum, nuclear and Palantir are all red — Nvidia's basically holding the index up by itself. Our 3x core is flat and sits right on its trend line; I'm holding it but a decisive break lower is the first thing I'd cut.

10:45am — calm hold, all protected. The day's real story: a report that **China may soon be cleared to buy Nvidia's top chips** (the H200) sent **Nvidia +2%** to $207 — but it's an Nvidia-*specific* pop, the rest of the chip group is still soft. We already own Nvidia through the 3x core and the little 2x position, so we're along for it; a whole Nvidia share is $11 more than our spare cash, so I can't cleanly add it. Best thing I *can* buy with the buffer is still **SoFi** — it's green while Rocket Lab, Palantir and the quantum names are all red, which is exactly the relative strength I want. One tap on SoFi and the cash is working; nothing else to do.

10:12am — market's open and the core **recovered the whole soft open**: the Nasdaq bounced back above its trend line and our 3x core is back to about **even** (~$1,002 equity). Chips are still **selling off** underneath (profit-taking after a huge first half) but the broad index is firmer, so the core stays. **SoFi** popped then faded to ~$19.07 — still green and above its breakout, so the one-tap's now a slightly *better* entry (buy near $19.07, hard exit $16.80). Everything's protected; one tap on SoFi puts the last of the cash to work.

9:45am — **SoFi popped ~5%** at the open and ran past our planned buy, so I bumped the one-tap up to catch the breakout near here, hard exit $16.80. Meanwhile chips are **selling off** hard (AI-spending jitters + a new China AI-chip headline) — our 3x-Nasdaq core is holding its trend line so it stays, but I'm watching it closely. Tap SoFi if you want the last of the cash working.

9:08am — ~20 minutes to the open and the tape's still **soft**: chips are down ahead of SK Hynix's giant Nasdaq listing and the 10-year yield's near 4.5%, so our 3x-Nasdaq core is off about **$10** pre-market. That's a normal wiggle — the hard exit sits way down at $65. Still **long** and protected, and your **SoFi** one-tap is ready to put the last ~20% cash to work at the open. Nothing for you to do.
